scope_boundary: synthetic costed-route, readiness-gate, and replacement-transaction fixture behavior only; no routing accuracy, readiness-engine behavior, residual-ledger storage, deployed quarantine, rollback execution, runtime monitoring, MoECOT replay, benchmark performance, or source-derived support-state promotion
transition_reason: The repository now has exact source mappings for the Readiness Gates claim, public schemas for costed route, readiness gate, and replacement transaction records, a synthetic readiness/residual gate harness with valid and expected-invalid fixtures, and proof traceability for finite readiness-gate predicates. Those artifacts support the design discipline around field identity matching, candidate-route selection, authority scope, blocked-route refusal, fallback preservation, route verification, non-stale evidence, regression floors, residual escrow, quarantine, rollback dry-run receipts, monitor status, and approval presence. They still do not establish routing accuracy, a deployed readiness engine, residual-ledger storage, deployed quarantine behavior, rollback execution, runtime monitoring, MoECOT replay, benchmark performance, or runtime safety.
required_artifacts: route-selection traces over representative route candidates with expected outcomes
readiness-engine execution trace that writes and rejects gate records under stale, failed, and authority-widening cases
residual-ledger storage and escrow replay tests
quarantine execution trace for failed selected routes with fallback preservation
rollback execution and monitor-status tests beyond dry-run receipt fields
MoECOT or prototype replay packet only if runtime-readiness claims are made
independent review of source interpretation adequacy
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
negative_results: schemas validate record shape rather than deployed readiness behavior
route-selection traces, readiness-engine execution, residual-ledger storage, deployed quarantine, rollback execution, runtime monitoring, and MoECOT replay are not implemented
downgrade_triggers: costed route or readiness schema drift
claim text interpreted as deployed readiness-engine or route-quality evidence
residual escrow claimed without storage or replay evidence
promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to the synthetic record-gate behavior that passed, or after route-selection traces, readiness-engine behavior, residual-ledger replay, deployed quarantine, rollback execution, runtime monitoring, and any MoECOT replay claims are recorded with accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: synthetic fixtures do not prove routing accuracy
schemas validate record shape rather than readiness-engine behavior
residual escrow fields do not prove a residual ledger stores or replays obligations
rollback dry-run receipt fields do not prove rollback execution
no independent routing, runtime, or source-interpretation review has been recorded
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent routing, runtime-safety, formal-methods, or source-interpretation audit
acceptance_blockers: no residual-ledger storage or replay test
non_claims: does not promote Readiness Gates above argument
does not prove residual-ledger storage or replay
does not prove deployed quarantine or rollback execution
does not prove runtime monitoring, MoECOT replay, benchmark performance, or runtime safety
